Verify each of the following 4 claims.
Each claim was extracted from its cited source by an AI system.

Research question: intermittent fasting effects on type 2 diabetes

Claim 1: All 29 studies showed safety of intermittent fasting and absence of adverse effects
  Source: The effect of intermittent fasting on mortality in patients with type 2 diabetes and metabolic disease with high cardiovascular risk: A systematic review (https://journals.viamedica.pl/clinical_diabetology/article/download/DK.a2021.0016/63451)
  Direct quote: "All studies showed the safety of intermittent fasting and absence of adverse effects"
  Fact category: finding

Claim 2: Restrictive dietary strategies including intermittent fasting may offer metabolic and performance-related benefits
  Source: Dietary Supplements and Popular Nutritional Strategies among Young Adults: Effectiveness, Safety, and Potential Health Risks–A Current Review of the Literature (https://apcz.umk.pl/QS/article/view/66978)
  Direct quote: "these approaches may offer metabolic and performance-related benefits"
  Fact category: finding

Claim 3: Common IF protocols include fasting 16-20 hours for 1-3 days/week, 24-hour fasts twice weekly, or alternate-day fasting with 75% energy restriction
  Source: Intermittent Fasting (IF) Promotes Longevity through Alterations of the Mammalian Target of Rapamycin (mTOR) and the Epigenome (https://openprairie.sdstate.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1012&context=biomicro_plan-b)
  Direct quote: "fasting for 16 to 20 hours of a 24 -hour day for 1 -3 days a week, fasting for 24 -hours two 

times a week, or alternate -day fasting"
  Fact category: protocol

Claim 4: Intermittent fasting is not superior to regular dieting; both produce equivalent weight loss and similar changes in blood pressure, cholesterol, and inflammation
  Source: Research review shows intermittent fasting works for weight loss, health changes (https://ahs.uic.edu/news-stories/research-review-shows-intermittent-fasting-works-for-weight-loss-health-changes/)
  Direct quote: "intermittent fasting is not better than regular dieting; both produce the same amount of weight loss and similar changes in blood pressure, cholesterol and inflammation"
  Fact category: finding


For each claim, assess whether it is plausibly supported by the cited source. If no direct quote is available, use the source title and URL as context.
